Defendant Cooley Llps Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery
Off Calendar May-06-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jonathan Patchen of Taylor & Company Law Offices LLP telephone number 415-788-8200 for Defendant.

Counsel for the Defendant stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem. The tentative ruling is uncontested. The Court adopted the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT COOLEY LLP'S Motion To Compel Discovery is GRANTED and DENIED in part, as follows. This ruling applies to Cooley's First and Second Set of Form Interrogatories. Form Interrogatory 4.1: GRANTED. The interrogatory requests information for "any policy if insurance through which you were or might be insured'" Granite's payment of a settlement is evidence that plaintiff was or might have been insured. Form Interrogatory 9.1: DENIED. Plaintiff has provided a code-complaint response. The response states "[a]ll plaintiffs seek the following damages'" Form Interrogatory 17.1: GRANTED. Requests for Production of Documents #1, 4, 5-8 (to TMG): GRANTED. The Court notes that the collateral source rules does not prevent Cooley from seeking discovery regarding payments by Granite. Defendant shall provide amended responses and produce responsive documents within 21 days of this order. Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/JPT)
